-module(solver).
-export([solve/3]).

%% solve(L, R, Directions) -> Count of "good" integers in [L, R]
solve(L, R, Directions) ->
    lists:sum([is_good(X, Directions) || X <- lists:seq(L, R)]).

%% is_good(X, Directions) -> 1 if X is good, 0 otherwise
is_good(X, Directions) ->
    Grid = create_grid(X),
    Path = trace_path(Directions),
    Sequence = get_sequence(Grid, Path),
    is_non_decreasing(Sequence) ->
        1;
    0.

%% create_grid(X) -> 4x4 grid (list of lists)
create_grid(X) ->
    BinaryString = io_lib:format("~p", [integer(X)]),
    PaddedString = string:pad_left(BinaryString, 16, "0"),
    Digits = lists:map(char_to_digit, string:chars(PaddedString)),
    
    %% Flattened list of 16 digits
    List16 = lists:take(16, Digits),
    
    %% Reshape into 4x4 grid
    Grid = lists:chunk(4, List16).

%% trace_path(Directions) -> list of 7 (row, col) pairs
trace_path(Directions) ->
    %% Start at (0, 0)
    InitialPosition = {0, 0},
    
    %% Apply 6 directions to get 6 subsequent positions
    PathSteps = lists:foldl(apply_direction, [InitialPosition], Directions),
    
    %% The sequence includes the starting cell, so we need 6 steps + 1 start = 7 positions
    PathSteps.

%% apply_direction(CurrentPosition, Direction, Accumulator) -> NewPosition
apply_direction({R, C}, 'D', Acc) ->
    {R + 1, C};
apply_direction({R, C}, 'R', Acc) ->
    {R, C + 1};
apply_direction(Pos, Dir, Acc) ->
    % Should not happen based on problem constraints
    Pos.

%% get_sequence(Grid, Path) -> List of 7 digits
get_sequence(Grid, Path) ->
    lists:map(get_digit_at, Path).

%% get_digit_at({R, C}, Grid) -> Digit at (R, C)
get_digit_at({R, C}, Grid) ->
    case lists:nth(R + 1, Grid) of
        Row ->
            lists:nth(C + 1, Row);
        _ ->
            % Error case: out of bounds, though path tracing should prevent this for 4x4
            -1
    end.

%% is_non_decreasing(Sequence) -> boolean
is_non_decreasing(Sequence) ->
    lists:all(fun(Idx) ->
        case {lists:nth(Idx, Sequence), lists:nth(Idx+1, Sequence)} of
            {Val1, Val2} -> Val1 =< Val2;
            _ -> true % Should not happen if length is 7
        end
    end, lists:seq(0, 5)).

%% Helper functions

%% char_to_digit(Char) -> Integer digit
char_to_digit(Char) ->
    case char_to_integer(Char) of
        {Ok, D} -> D;
        error -> 0 % Should not happen for digits '0'-'9'
    end.

%% string:pad_left(String, Length, PadChar) -> Padded String
string:pad_left(Str, Len, PadChar) ->
    Len =:= length(Str) -> Str;
    Padding = string:repeat(PadChar, Len - length(Str)),
    Padding ++ Str.

%% lists:chunk(Size, List) -> List of chunks of size Size
lists:chunk(Size, List) ->
    case List of
        [] -> [];
        _ ->
            Chunk = lists:take(Size, List),
            Rest = lists:drop(Size, List),
            [Chunk | lists:chunk(Size, Rest)]
    end.

%% lists:take(N, List) -> First N elements
lists:take(0, _) -> [];
lists:take(_N, []) -> [];
lists:take(N, [_|T]) ->
    [H|L] = lists:take(N-1, T),
    [H|L].

%% lists:drop(N, List) -> List after N elements
lists:drop(0, List) -> List;
lists:drop(_N, []) -> [];
lists:drop(N, [_|T]) -> lists:drop(N-1, T).

%% Erlang's standard lists:seq/2 is used for iteration range.
%% Integer arithmetic for sequence generation.
